Kql: datatable(k:string, v:long)[ "A",1, "a",2, "B",3, "b",4 ] | summarize s=sum(v), ml=make_list(v) by k | order by k asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT k, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS s, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS ml FROM (VALUES ('A', 1), ('a', 2), ('B', 3), ('b', 4)) AS t(k, v) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(x:long, y:long)[ 1,1, 1,2, 1,3, 2,1, 2,2, 3,1 ] | summarize inner=make_list(y), cnt=count() by x | summarize outer_lists=make_list(inner), totcnt=sum(cnt), grps=count()
Sql: SELECT COALESCE(LIST("inner"), []) AS outer_lists, COALESCE(SUM(cnt), 0) AS totcnt, COUNT(*) AS grps FROM (SELECT x, COALESCE(LIST(y), []) AS "inner", COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM (VALUES (1, 1), (1, 2), (1, 3), (2, 1), (2, 2), (3, 1)) AS t(x, y) GROUP BY ALL)

Kql: datatable(v:long)[ 1,2,3,4,5 ] | summarize a=avgif(v, true), b=avgif(v, false), c=sumif(v, false), d=countif(false), e=minif(v, false), f=maxif(v, false)
Sql: SELECT COALESCE(AVG(v) FILTER (WHERE TRUE), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS a, COALESCE(AVG(v) FILTER (WHERE FALSE), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS b, COALESCE(SUM(v) FILTER (WHERE FALSE), 0) AS c, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE FALSE) AS d, MIN(v) FILTER (WHERE FALSE) AS e, MAX(v) FILTER (WHERE FALSE) AS f FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3), (4), (5)) AS t(v)

Kql: datatable(g:bool, v:long)[ true,1, true,2, false,3, false,4, false,5 ] | summarize s=sum(v), c=count(), ml=make_list(v) by g | order by g asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT g, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS s, COUNT(*) AS c, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS ml FROM (VALUES (TRUE, 1), (TRUE, 2), (FALSE, 3), (FALSE, 4), (FALSE, 5)) AS t(g, v) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY g ASC

Kql: datatable(k:long, v:decimal)[ 1,decimal(1.5), 1,decimal(2.5), 2,decimal(3.5) ] | summarize s=sum(v), av=avg(v), mx=max(v), mn=min(v), ml=make_list(v) by k | order by k asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT k, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS s, COALESCE(AVG(v), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS av, MAX(v) AS mx, MIN(v) AS mn, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS ml FROM (VALUES (1, 1.5), (1, 2.5), (2, 3.5)) AS t(k, v) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(x:long)[ 9223372036854775806, 1, 9223372036854775807 ] | summarize mx=max(x), mn=min(x), av=avg(x), ml=make_list(x)
Sql: SELECT MAX(x) AS mx, MIN(x) AS mn, COALESCE(AVG(x), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS av, COALESCE(LIST(x), []) AS ml FROM (VALUES (9223372036854775806), (1), (9223372036854775807)) AS t(x)

Kql: datatable(g:string, v:long)[ "a",10, "a",20, "b",30 ] | summarize amx=arg_max(v, *) by g | order by g asc
Detail: Unsupported expression StarExpression

Kql: datatable(k:long, v:long)[ 1,1, 1,2, 1,3, 2,4, 2,5, 3,6 ] | summarize n=count(), s=sum(v), avgv=avg(v), lst=make_list(v) by k | where n >= 2 | extend isbig=(s>5) | order by s desc | take 5
Sql: SELECT *, (s > 5) AS isbig FROM (SELECT k, COUNT(*) AS n, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS s, COALESCE(AVG(v), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS avgv, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS lst FROM (VALUES (1, 1), (1, 2), (1, 3), (2, 4), (2, 5), (3, 6)) AS t(k, v) GROUP BY ALL) WHERE n >= 2 ORDER BY s DESC LIMIT 5

Kql: datatable(v:long)[ 1,2,3,4,5,6 ] | summarize a=make_list(v, 3), b=make_set(v, 2)
Sql: SELECT COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS a, COALESCE(LIST(DISTINCT v), []) AS b FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3), (4), (5), (6)) AS t(v)

Kql: datatable(k:long, x:real)[ 1,1.0, 1,2.0, 1,3.0, 1,4.0, 2,100.0 ] | summarize var_check=variance(x), stdev_check=stdev(x), sumsq=sum(x*x), n=count(), avgsq=avg(x*x) by k | order by k asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT k, VAR_SAMP(x) AS var_check, STDDEV_SAMP(x) AS stdev_check, COALESCE(SUM(x * x), 0) AS sumsq, COUNT(*) AS n, COALESCE(AVG(x * x), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS avgsq FROM (VALUES (1, 1.0), (1, 2.0), (1, 3.0), (1, 4.0), (2, 100.0)) AS t(k, x) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(g:string, v:dynamic)[ "p",dynamic([1,2]), "p",dynamic([3,4]), "q",dynamic(null), "q",dynamic([5]) ] | summarize ml=make_list(v), mln=make_list_with_nulls(v) by g | order by g asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT g, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS ml, COALESCE(LIST(v), []) AS mln FROM (VALUES ('p', LIST_VALUE(1, 2)), ('p', LIST_VALUE(3, 4)), ('q', NULL), ('q', LIST_VALUE(5))) AS t(g, v) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY g ASC

Kql: datatable(k:long, a:long, b:long, c:string)[ 1,5,10,"x", 1,5,20,"y", 1,3,30,"z", 2,9,40,"w" ] | summarize arg_max(a, b, c), arg_min(a, c) by k | order by k asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT k, MAX(a) AS a, ARG_MAX(b, a) AS b, ARG_MAX(c, a) AS c, MIN(a) AS a1, ARG_MIN(c, a) AS c1 FROM (VALUES (1, 5, 10, 'x'), (1, 5, 20, 'y'), (1, 3, 30, 'z'), (2, 9, 40, 'w')) AS t(k, a, b, c) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(v:int)[ 1,2,3,4,5 ] | summarize s=sum(v*1.0), m=avg(todouble(v)), lst=make_list(v*2), prod=make_list(v*v), neg=make_set(-v)
Sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(v * 1.0), 0) AS s, COALESCE(AVG(COALESCE(TRY_CAST(v AS DOUBLE), TRY_CAST(TRY_CAST(v AS BOOLEAN) AS DOUBLE))), 'nan'::DOUBLE) AS m, COALESCE(LIST(v * 2), []) AS lst, COALESCE(LIST(v * v), []) AS prod, COALESCE(LIST(DISTINCT (-v)), []) AS neg FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3), (4), (5)) AS t(v)

Kql: datatable(g:long, v:long)[ 1,10, 1,20, 2,30, 3,40, 3,50 ] | summarize cnt=count() by g | summarize bag=make_bag(pack(tostring(g), cnt)), histlist=make_list(pack("g", g, "c", cnt))
Sql: SELECT histogram(json_object(TRY_CAST(g AS TEXT), cnt)) AS bag, COALESCE(LIST(json_object('g', g, 'c', cnt)), []) AS histlist FROM (SELECT g, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM (VALUES (1, 10), (1, 20), (2, 30), (3, 40), (3, 50)) AS t(g, v) GROUP BY ALL)

Kql: datatable(k:long, v:long)[ 1,10, 1,-10, 1,20, 2,-30, 2,30 ] | summarize possum=sumif(v, v>0), negsum=sumif(v, v<0), poscnt=countif(v>0), absmax=max(abs(v)), netsum=sum(v) by k | order by k asc
Sql: SELECT * FROM (SELECT k, COALESCE(SUM(v) FILTER (WHERE v > 0), 0) AS possum, COALESCE(SUM(v) FILTER (WHERE v < 0), 0) AS negsum, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE v > 0) AS poscnt, MAX(GREATEST(v, -(v))) AS absmax, COALESCE(SUM(v), 0) AS netsum FROM (VALUES (1, 10), (1, -10), (1, 20), (2, -30), (2, 30)) AS t(k, v) GROUP BY ALL) ORDER BY k ASC

Kql: datatable(x:long)[ 1,2,3 ] | summarize total=count() | extend doubled=total*2 | summarize final=sum(doubled), lst=make_list(doubled), mx=max(total)
Sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(doubled), 0) AS final, COALESCE(LIST(doubled), []) AS lst, MAX(total) AS mx FROM (SELECT *, total * 2 AS doubled FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM (VALUES (1), (2), (3)) AS t(x)))
